feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Build
feed.subtitle: SAP Build Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: SAP Build apps action. Can't read JSON from API
entry.content:
When running a test on an action in SAP Build I get the following error.

{
"code": 12000,
"message": "Execution of action failed",
"correlationId": "27932dd7-91bf-4fe8-4402-19710945fc43",
"details": [
{ "code": 12000,
"message": "Failed to create action output for action get and response code 200: Cannot convert undefined or null to object",
"severity": "ERROR"
}
]

[
  {
    "description": "xxxxx",
    "metadata": {
      " below_unit": " -",
      " global_trade_item_number": 186,
      " in_unit": " -",
      " lead_time": 15,
      " max_energy_consumption": " -",
      " max_flow": 0,
      " max_rec_head": 0,
      " name": " Condenser Cleaner 600ml Box Lbl",
      " remotely": " -",
      " site_id": " BR1000",
      " sound_db": 0,
      " suction_lift": 0,
      " system_type": " -",
      " trunking": " -",
      "product_id": "6755",
      "score": 0.8306616847524148
    },
    "similarity_score": 0.8306616847524148
  },
  {
    "description": "xxxxxx",
    "metadata": {
      " below_unit": " -",
      " global_trade_item_number": 185,
      " in_unit": " -",
      " lead_time": 15,
      " max_energy_consumption": " -",
      " max_flow": 0,
      " max_rec_head": 0,
      " name": " Condenser Cleaner 1L Box Lbl",
      " remotely": " -",
      " site_id": " BR1000",
      " sound_db": 0,
      " suction_lift": 0,
      " system_type": " -",
      " trunking": " -",
      "product_id": "6725",
      "score": 0.823491177142724
    },
    "similarity_score": 0.823491177142724
  },
  {
    "description": "xxxxx",
    "metadata": {
      " below_unit": " -",
      " global_trade_item_number": 184,
      " in_unit": " -",
      " lead_time": 15,
      " max_energy_consumption": " -",
      " max_flow": 0,
      " max_rec_head": 0,
      " name": " Condenser Cleaner Conc 1L Box Lbl",
      " remotely": " -",
      " site_id": " BR1000",
      " sound_db": 0,
      " suction_lift": 0,
      " system_type": " -",
      " trunking": " -",
      "product_id": "6695",
      "score": 0.8102495639772882
    },
    "similarity_score": 0.8102495639772882
  },
  {
    "description": "xxxxx",
    "metadata": {
      " below_unit": " -",
      " global_trade_item_number": 187,
      " in_unit": " -",
      " lead_time": 15,
      " max_energy_consumption": " -",
      " max_flow": 0,
      " max_rec_head": 0,
      " name": " Universal Coil Cleaner Conc 1L Box Lbl",
      " remotely": " -",
      " site_id": " BR1000",
      " sound_db": 0,
      " suction_lift": 0,
      " system_type": " -",
      " trunking": " -",
      "product_id": "6785",
      "score": 0.8033885945712105
    },
    "similarity_score": 0.8033885945712105
  },
  {
    "description": "xxxxxx",
    "metadata": {
      " below_unit": " -",
      " global_trade_item_number": 139,
      " in_unit": " -",
      " lead_time": 15,
      " max_energy_consumption": " -",
      " max_flow": 0,
      " max_rec_head": 0,
      " name": " Conden Clean & Degr 600ml Label ES REV2",
      " remotely": " -",
      " site_id": " BR1000",
      " sound_db": 0,
      " suction_lift": 0,
      " system_type": " -",
      " trunking": " -",
      "product_id": "7060",
      "score": 0.7943730599435553
    },
    "similarity_score": 0.7943730599435553
  }
]

The body of the reponse also converts the whole the payload into a string.

The above works fine when creating a data integration in SAP Build apps.

Also, simpler JSON seems to work fine in a SAP Build Action.

[
    {
        "xxx",
        "product_id": null,
        "product_name": null,
        "similarity_score": 0.8306556940078735
    },
    {
        "xxx",
        "product_id": null,
        "product_name": null,
        "similarity_score": 0.823538373497823
    },
    {
        "xxxxx",
        "product_id": null,
        "product_name": null,
        "similarity_score": 0.8102967256130754
    },
    {
        "product_description": "xxxx",
        "product_id": null,
        "product_name": null,
        "similarity_score": 0.8034826272459229
    }
]

Please advise as our API needs to be called from a SAP Build Action (to be used in a process automation), a SAP Build app or anywhere else.

entry.title: SAP BPA application shows Blank Page
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have successfully subscribed for SAP BPA and it got created as well & then completed the role assignment also. But while accessing the BPA application, it is showing blank page.

Could you please suggest how to fix this.

Many Thanks

entry.title: Unable to laod SAP Build Process Automation - In Maintenance Mode message
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

When i try to open my SAP Build Process Automation, I'm getting the below attached message.

 

Any idea why i'm getting this? or how much more time it would take? Can i do something to fix this?

I was trying to complete the following tutorial when it started - Create a CAP Service with BAS Productivity Tools and I had completed the setup of SAP Build Apps with Booster when the Maintenance message appeared.

Thanks in advance

Adam

entry.title: Loop/Iterate in the SAP Build Process Autoamtion Process flow
entry.content:
I am looking for a way to use loops or iterate calling an action in the process.

I am aware of the looping fucntionality and other conditions available in the automation, but currently I am looking for loops in the Process alone, that way not having to use automation/desktop agent.


Please let me know if the functionality is already present or if there can be any workarounds that I can use.
Thanks in advance

entry.title: SAP Build project isn't displayed
entry.content:
Hi,

In the last week, I can't see my project.

I created an automation project, but in the last week, when I click on my project in the SAP build lobby I am getting a blank page, where before I used to get the list of the components of my projects.

It is the same, no matter what project I am trying to open.

I tried my trial account and it is the same, and my colleague are also having the same experience.

SAP Build Process Automation

entry.title: Error - Failed to start outlook, Internal Instance of outlook is null.
entry.content: I am creating a simple automation of outlook using this https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/spa-create-outlook-automation.html#6d46ac9d-1797-4178-808d-8438c019d13b . But i'm getting error "Error - Failed to start outlook, Internal Instance of outlook is null. " when i'm testing the automation.

entry.title: I'm facing issue while registering for External Authentication in SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content: I'm facing issue while registering for External Authentication in SAP Build Process Automation. It returns a Blank screen and registration doesn't complete.

entry.title: Issue displaying data in item list in Build Apps
entry.content:
Hi,

I need a hand regarding an app I'm creating in Build Apps.

I'm building a pretty straight-forward app for our clients to approve their own invoices. It is composed of 4 screens (Authentication, Invoice list, Invoice Details and Account Balance). Basically, after authenticating, the Invoice List page appears, then the user selects an invoice and is taken to the Invoice Details page where she/he can either approve or reject the invoice. If she/he approves the invoice, the number and amount of the invoice appear on the Account Balance page. Each time an invoice is approved, the related data is added to the Balance page.

So I manage to display an approved invoice in the item list of the Balance page. However, when I approve another invoice, it is not added to the previously approved one on the Balance page: it replaces it.

Below you will find several screenshots showing how I configured both Invoice Details page and Balance page: could you please give a look to see what I am missing please? I also joined a short video.

Here is the app variable I defined, named “approvedInvoice” (list of 3 objects), where I want to store the data of the invoices that are being approved:

Here is the logic flow I added to the Approve button ("Set app variable", where I added the app variable I created in the properties and assign the value with a custom list):

When I click on the Custom list of the Assigned value from the above screen, here is what I configured (DATA ENTITY TEST being my data source, the one from which I get all the data of my invoices that are display in the Invoices page):

Here is how I configured the Open Page properties ("approvedInvoice" being my page parameter):

And here you can see how I configured my list item in the Balance page:

See the video here for better understanding (N.B.: as you will notice, I'm currently displaying the client name in place of the amount, it is just temporary and does not change the problem raised in this post):

This is what happens when I approve more than one invoice: approved invoices don't pile up, only the latest approved one is displayed...

Would you be so kind and give me just a hint to solve this please? I'm stuck on this for a week now and it is blocking the whole project...

Many thanks beforehand for your help and I'm looking forward to reading you.

BR,

LM.

entry.title: Looking for app developers who monitor apps they deployed
entry.content:
Hi,

We're working on a new dashboard for application monitoring for frontend, backend, and fullstack developers. 

I'm looking for developers who monitor the application they deployed for short interview to better understand what info you would expect to see and what actions you would expect to be available for you.

No need to prepare anything. Only meet for 30 min interview and impact on dashboard design.

Your feedback is highly appreciated.

Ravid (ravid.ellenbogen@sap.com)

entry.title: Looking for app developers who monitor apps they deployed
entry.content:
Hi,

We're working on a new dashboard for application monitoring for frontend, backend, and fullstack developers. 

I'm looking for developers who monitor the application they deployed for short interview to better understand what info you would expect to see and what actions you would expect to be available for you.

No need to prepare anything. Only meet for 30 min interview and impact on dashboard design.

Your feedback is highly appreciated.

Ravid (ravid.ellenbogen@sap.com)

entry.title: CORS (Cross-Origin Resource Sharing) Error in SAP Build Apps - OData API Call
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I'm experiencing a CORS (Cross-Origin Resource Sharing) error while calling an OData API from SAP Build Apps. Despite configuring the API and app settings, I continue to encounter this issue on Web-browser.
 
Error Details:
  • Error Message: 
  • Product: SAP Build Apps 
  • OData API Version: V2.0
  • Browser/Platform: Mozilla/5.0
Steps Taken:
  • Verified API and app credentials
  • Tested API calls using tools like Postman (successful)
  • Configuring proxy settings in SAP Build
  • Adding CORS headers manually in the API requests
  • Using CORS-enabled libraries
Question:
Can anyone provide guidance on resolving this CORS error in SAP Build Apps? Are there specific settings or configurations I'm missing? Specifically:
  • How to configure SAP Build to handle CORS requests?
  • Best practices for handling CORS in SAP Build apps?
  • Any known workarounds or third-party libraries that can help resolve this issue?
Thank you for your assistance.
 
Best regards,
Aman K.

entry.title: How we connect SAP Build Apps with PostgreSQL.
entry.content: I am created an application with SAP Build App. But, I don't know how to connect SAP Build App with PostgreSQL.

entry.title: Error in SAP Build apps Build service 4.11.167
entry.content:
I suddenly get an error when trying to make a web build in SAP Build apps. I then tried to Build an older app that I havent touched since I created it and it gives the same error message. I cant make any Build at the moment. I am using the Community edition. I have searched the forum and found others getting the same error message but not any solutions except not making copies of an app but instead saving them as new projects. I cant see that I have that option in the Community edition.Tried to make a build with an app that I know is not copied but it does not work either. Please help! Thanks!

 

AB version: 2.13.4
COMMAND: 

NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=12240 NEXT_SUPPORT_RELATIVE_PATHS=true USER_METERING_ENABLED=true SHOW_SUBSCRIPTION_VERIFICATION_NOTIFICATION=false SUBSCRIPTION_VERIFICATION_NOTIFICATION_DISMISSIBLE=true  npxrnv export -p web -s standalone -c standalone --ci --yes --skipRnvCheck --packageManager yarn --template @sapappgyver/orchestra-template-standalone@4.11.167 -r 

FAILED with ERROR: 

Command failed with exit code 1: npx rnv export -p web -s standalone -c standalone --ci --yes --skipRnvCheck --packageManager yarn --template @sapappgyver/orchestra-template-standalone@4.11.167 -r

entry.title: Why is a SAP Build APPS app 10 times faster on an Android device then on a web server?
entry.content: I have built and app and tested it both on a webbserver and as an app on an Android device. When I run it on the tablet it is approx 10 times faster then when I run the app on the webbserver. I tested it on the local webbserver. It is also slow when I launch the app in the preview mode in SAP Build apps. Does anyone know if I can do something to fix this? Thanks?

entry.title: Problem with request body mapper and how to write the code
entry.content:
Hi,

I built and app and it works perfect on webb and in the beginning also on an Android tablet. Suddenly the functions that made calls to oData API stopped working on all tablets at the same time. I have not been able to solve this so now I decided to change the data sources to REST api instead since they have worked perfect. Problem is that when I for example wants to make a call and GET records sending conditions I cant seem to write the correct code in the request body mapper. I get all records evry time when i test. For example a simple call with just one filter condition like this:

ENCODE_JSON({ "conditions": [ { "field": "U_DocEntry", "operator": "=", "value": 478 } ]}) OR

ENCODE_JSON({"U_DocEntry":478})

I make calls to SAP Business one service layer and in this case it is a UDT(own table). Can someone please tell me what I am doing wrong? Thanks!

entry.title: BAPI exception : Prerequisites are missing
entry.content:
Hi,

When using Set SAP Connection (Basic) BAPI activity in sap build process automation i am getting this error "BAPI exception: Prerequisites are missing". Can you please let me know what exactly i need to do now to overcome this error and make a successful connection with SAP through BAPI.....

entry.title: SAP Build Apps - Visual Cloud Functions - "RBAC: access denied" on retrive, create
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have created a Visual cloud function project in SAP Build Apps and created an entity. When I tried to retrieve the entity via Browse Data  or add a row manually via Browse Data I get the below error.

 

 

The role and user configuration looks ok to me. Tried deleting and reconfiguring the roles but did not help.

I made sure the App is deployed after changing the roles.

I am using the same email id to login. 

 

 

Any pointers on this is highly appreciated.
@Dan_Wroblewski @JoeBinkley @beapasch

entry.title: Combining Data from Multiple APIs in SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
Hi amazing SAP enthusiasts!

I hope you’re all having a great day! I’m diving into a project using SAP Build Apps and could really use your expertise on a particular challenge I’m facing.

I’m trying to display data in a single table that pulls from two different APIs. API1 gives me two of the fields I need, while API2 provides the other two. My goal is to combine these fields into one cohesive table.

If anyone has experience with this, I’d love to hear your tips or best practices! Are there specific functions or approaches within SAP Build Apps that can help me achieve this integration smoothly

Looking forward to your insights!

entry.title: How we can filter Odata data in SAP BUILD APP.
entry.content:
I got data in SAP BUILD APP with Odata URL. But now I want to know how we can filter only selected
data (UserID, Employee Name) in SAP BUILD APP.

entry.title: Re: CORS (Cross-Origin Resource Sharing) Error in SAP Bui...
entry.content:
Hi @_Aman_ 

It looks like the CORS error might be due to an issue with the link you’re using, as it points to a cloud-hosted application. I recommend setting up SAP BTP (Business Technology Platform) destinations to manage connections more securely and avoid CORS issues.

  1. Make sure your destination is correctly set with the proper URL and authentication.
  2. Use transaction code RZ10 to configure CORS settings in your S/4HANA system, allowing your OData API to accept requests from your application.
  3. If you manage the OData service, make sure the necessary CORS headers are included.

Testing through SAP Build Apps after these changes should help. Let me know if you need further assistance!
